title: DOJ reviewing potentially classified docs at Biden center
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is reviewing a batch of potentially classified documents found in the Washington office space of President Joe Biden's former institute, the White House said Monday.

title: Having elected House speaker, Republicans try governing
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Electing the House speaker may have been the easy part.

title: How Republicans are transforming the House in the majority
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lawmakers no longer have to walk through metal detectors before gaining access to the House floor. And any time they do vote, they will have to do so in person — no more voting by proxy from home.

title: Mexico may accept more migrants expelled by US
shortDescription: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Andres Manuel López Obrador said Monday in the leadup to this week’s summit of North American leaders that he would consider accepting more migrants than previously announced under President Joe Biden’s plan to turn away people from four nations who cross illegally into the United States.

title: Brazil and Jan. 6 in US: Parallel attacks, but not identical
shortDescription: Enraged protesters broke into government buildings that are the very symbol of their country’s democracy. Driven by conspiracy theories about their candidate's loss in the last election, they smashed windows, sifted through the desks of lawmakers and trashed the highest offices in the land in a rampage that lasted hours before order could be restored.

title: US says Iran may be ‘contributing’ to war crimes in Ukraine
shortDescription: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that Iran's sale of lethal drones to Russia for use in its ongoing invasion of Ukraine means the country may be "contributing to widespread war crimes."

title: Republicans challenge New Mexico redistricting after loss
shortDescription: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The New Mexico Supreme Court is scheduled to hear oral arguments Monday in a legal challenge to a congressional map that divvies up a politically conservative region of the state.

title: Georgia grand jury ends probe of Trump, 2020 election
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — The special grand jury in Atlanta that has been investigating whether then-President Donald Trump and his allies committed any crimes while trying to overturn his 2020 election loss in Georgia has finished its work, bringing the case closer to possible criminal charges against Trump and others.

title: Kemp done being underestimated, aims to steer GOP past Trump
shortDescription: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp is done being underestimated.Having vanquished both a Donald Trump-backed Republican challenger and Democratic star Stacey Abrams to win reelection, Kemp is looking to expand his influence in his second term, free from the caricature of the gun-toting, pickup-driving, migrant-catching country boy that emerged during his first campaign for governor.

title: US law based on anti-Latino racism fuels immigration fight
shortDescription: LAS VEGAS (AP) — As thousands of children were taken from their parents at the southern border during a Trump administration crackdown on illegal crossings, a federal public defender in San Diego set out to find new strategies to go after the longstanding deportation law fueling the family separations.

title: GOP’s White files to seek reelection as Mississippi auditor
shortDescription: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — Republican Shad White has filed to seek another term as state auditor in Mississippi.White was first appointed to the job by then-Gov. Phil Bryant in 2018 after Stacey Pickering stepped down.

title: Washington lawmakers convene for 105-day session
shortDescription: OLYMPIA, Wash. (AP) — Lawmakers in Washington state returned to Olympia on Monday to begin a 105-day legislative session, the first one fully in person since the COVID-19 pandemic forced lawmakers and the public to often meet and vote virtually.

title: Minnesota elections chief seeks to make voting even easier
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Minnesota's chief elections officer called on state lawmakers Monday to make it easier for residents to vote while protecting elections officials from threats and intimidation.

title: ND considers legal sports betting outside of tribal casinos
shortDescription: Amid ongoing efforts to legalize sports betting in North Dakota — and expand it beyond the tribal casinos, where it's already allowed — a House panel heard arguments Monday over a measure to let voters decide the issue next year.

title: Hobbs focuses on border, schools in 1st speech to lawmakers
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs on Monday praised President Joe Biden's weekend trip to the U.S.-Mexico border and his stepped-up efforts to combat illegal immigration.A week after the newly elected Democrat took office, Hobbs looked for balance between get-tough border hawks and immigration advocates focused on a humanitarian approach in her first state of the state address to Arizona's Republican-controlled Legislature.

title: Arkansas lawmakers say education top issue as session starts
shortDescription: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Arkansas lawmakers kicked off their annual legislative session Monday, with Republicans enjoying expanded supermajorities and embracing an agenda by incoming Gov.

title: GOP lawmaker turns on Democrat he helped win Pa. speakership
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The Pennsylvania state House Republican who recently nominated and voted for Democratic Rep. Mark Rozzi to serve as speaker wrote him Monday to say Rozzi was waffling on whether he will register as an independent and therefore should resign.

title: Louisiana state treasurer says he will run for governor
shortDescription: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana State Treasurer John Schroder told supporters Monday that he is officially running for governor. Louisiana’s current Gov. John Bel Edwards, a Democrat, is term-limited, making him unable to run this year — opening a huge opportunity for the GOP to capture the state’s top government post.

title: South Dakota $423M surplus will be top issue at Legislature
shortDescription: The South Dakota Legislature’s nine-week session is slated to begin Tuesday with all eyes on what lawmakers might do with the state’s $423 million surplus.Gov. Kristi Noem wants to use the money to cover repealing the state sales tax on groceries, a plan some of the state’s staunchest Republicans oppose.

title: Idaho governor touts funding for education, public safety
shortDescription: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho Gov. Brad Little announced proposals for property tax relief, education funding and efforts to fight the influx of illegal fentanyl during his annual State of the State address on Monday.

title: Venezuela orders arrest of exiled lawmakers fighting Maduro
shortDescription: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela's socialist government has ordered the arrest of three exiled former lawmakers at the forefront of renewed efforts to unseat President Nicolas Maduro.Dinorah Figuera leads an all-female team selected last week by fellow opposition politicians to lead the National Assembly that was voted into office in 2015.

title: Abortion, roads, trans youth top Tennessee lawmaking session
shortDescription: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — As Tennessee lawmakers head into an annual legislative session on Tuesday, some fights are already underway — over whether the state's strict abortion ban needs exceptions, if express toll lanes and electric vehicle fee hikes can help solve roadwork needs, and how restrictive Republicans want the law to be regarding transgender youth health treatment.

title: Russia launches criminal probes on prominent Kremlin critics
shortDescription: Russian authorities on Monday announced parallel criminal probes against a famous actor critical of the war in Ukraine and a philanthropist who supports the Russian opposition, the latest in a months-long, sweeping crackdown on dissent.

title: Japan PM in Paris to discuss Ukraine, climate, food security
shortDescription: PARIS (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida started a weeklong trip to Europe and North America in Paris where he held talks Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on key issues including North Korea and the war in Ukraine.

title: Oklahoma governor, elected leaders sworn into office
shortDescription: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt and other statewide elected officials took their oaths of office on Monday ahead of a legislative session in which lawmakers are expected to consider cutting taxes amid record revenue collections.

title: Iowa lawmakers turn to taxes, guns, private school funding
shortDescription: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Iowa Legislature got back to work Monday with Republicans holding even larger majorities in both chambers and goals of approving more tax cuts, further easing gun restrictions and spending public money to fund private schools.

title: UN extends critical aid from Turkey to Syria’s rebel north
shortDescription: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council voted unanimously Monday to keep a key border crossing from Turkey to Syria’s rebel-held northwest open for critical aid deliveries for another six months.

title: For Trump, Georgia election case just one of many legal woes
shortDescription: A special grand jury investigating efforts by Donald Trump and his allies to overturn his 2020 election defeat in Georgia has finished its report, a major development in a case that's on a long list of legal problems for the former president.

title: Biden seeks Mexico's AMLO help in stopping deadly fentanyl
shortDescription: Joe Biden kicks off the North American Leaders' Summit asking Mexican President Andrés Manuel López Obrador for help tackling the fentanyl crisis.

title: House to vote on new rules, including making it easier to oust speaker
shortDescription: The rules would give more power to individual members, allow for aggressive probes of the administration, and make it harder to raise spending.

title: McCarthy's new GOP ditches House metal detectors in one of first acts
shortDescription: Republicans removed metal detectors outside the House floor that were erected in the wake of Jan. 6 in their first act of power last week.

title: Gaetz, Rogers make peace after House floor confrontation
shortDescription: Republican Mike Rogers created one of the viral moments of the historic speaker election last week when he charged at fellow GOP Rep. Matt Gaetz.

title: Georgia grand jury completes Trump probe
shortDescription: Fulton County Superior Court Judge Robert McBurney set a Jan. 24 hearing to determine whether the report would be made public.

title: Charging decisions on Trump loom large in Atlanta. What we know.
shortDescription: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis' team of prosecutors and investigators has guided the grand jury's work since it was empaneled in May.

title: 70,000 US deaths: Can Biden, Mexico agree on fighting fentanyl?
shortDescription: President Joe Biden heads to Mexico to discuss the lethal fentanyl threat with Mexico's president. Analysts say the U.S. needs much more cooperation.

title: Despite new policies, Biden immigration plans may be tied up in court
shortDescription: As Biden heads into a summit with his counterparts from Mexico and Canada, his immigration policies are increasingly being tied up  in federal courts.

title: Biden faces pleas for help on migrant crisis as he visits El Paso border
shortDescription: Joe Biden's trip to El Paso marks his first visit to the U.S.-Mexico border since he became president two years ago.

title: From immigration to fentanyl, Biden to highlight US issues in Mexico
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is meeting with Mexico and Canada as the U.S. faces record levels of migrants and rising fentanyl overdoses.

title: How Trump could shape the race to replace Stabenow
shortDescription: Republicans say they will compete for Debbie Stabenow's open Senate seat in 2024, but will Donald Trump help or hurt that effort.

title: Biden to seek mayors' help to implement 'new opportunities'
shortDescription: President Joe Biden invites U.S. mayors to the White House on Jan. 20, his second anniversary in office, to discuss bipartisan work on key priorities.

title: In echo of Jan. 6, Brazil protesters storm their Congress, high court
shortDescription: The uprising comes after supporters of far-right President Jair Bolsonaro wouldn't accept his defeat by President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva.

title: US appeals court blocks ban on rapid-fire ‘bump stocks’
shortDescription: A Trump administration ban on bump stocks was blocked by an appeals court. The device was banned after used by the gunman in the 2017 Las Vegas attack

title: Homeless crisis, a city overwhelmed: What Biden may see at El Paso border
shortDescription: If given the chance, the president will see the migrant crisis among people sleeping under Red Cross blankets outside Sacred Heart Catholic Church.

title: McCarthy's next battle: Rallying GOP behind deal he made with hard-liners
shortDescription: GOP Rep. Nancy Mace, a staunch Kevin McCarthy supporter, said she was "on the fence" about the rules package the speaker negotiated with hard-liners.

title: How a new Congress will change the direction of the country: What we know
shortDescription: Speaker Kevin McCarthy and the GOP have control of the House of Representatives. Meanwhile, Democrats hold the edge in the Senate.

title: Five Albuquerque Democrats' homes, offices targeted by shootings
shortDescription: Albuquerque police are investigating whether shootings targeting Democratic lawmakers homes and offices are connected or politically motivated.

title: Slain Jan. 6 rioter's mother arrested on Capitol attack anniversary
shortDescription: Video footage of Witthoeft's arrest shows Capitol Police officers telling her and other protestors to move to the "sidewalk now, or you're under arrest."

title: Norman Pattis, Alex Jones' lawyer, loses law license for 6 months
shortDescription: Norman Pattis, Alex Jones' attorney, is also representing Proud Boy Joseph Biggs in an ongoing seditious conspiracy trial in D.C.

title: Recap: On marathon 15th ballot, McCarthy wins House speaker vote
shortDescription: On a marathon fourth day, after a chaotic week for House Republicans, GOP Leader Rep. Kevin McCarthy won speaker of the House. Members were sworn in.

title: McCarthy elected House speaker on 15th vote
shortDescription: The GOP leader Kevin McCarthy was elected House speaker after historic 15 rounds of votes.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: House G.O.P. Races to Line Up Votes for Rules After McCarthy Concessions
shortDescription: Some rank-and-file Republicans balked at a package of proposed changes to House rules that would enshrine concessions the speaker made to win support from ultraconservative members.By Catie Edmondson

title: The Republicans Are Putting Trump Out to Pasture
shortDescription: The former president had a moment, but now the sun is setting on that moment.By Charles M. Blow

title: Republicans Prepare New Rules, but Fixing Congress Isn’t So Easy
shortDescription: History suggests that the procedural plans of the new House Republican majority are likely to lead to more gridlock and legislative instability, not less.By Carl Hulse

title: House Republicans Preparing Broad Inquiry Into F.B.I. and Security Agencies
shortDescription: Republicans plan to create a special subcommittee, led by a Trump ally, with a mandate to scrutinize open criminal investigations and classified intelligence.By Charlie Savage and Luke Broadwater

title: In Speaker Fight’s Final Hours, Arm-Twisting, Flaring Tempers and Calls From Trump
shortDescription: Speaker Kevin McCarthy’s slog to his post ended with a remarkably public show of intraparty strife that played out in a history-making overnight session.By Catie Edmondson

title: McCarthy Wins House Speakership After 15 Votes
shortDescription: Representative Kevin McCarthy of California delivered a speech that focused on unity. Then, members-elect and delegates were sworn in.By House Tv

title: Kevin McCarthy and the Return of the Pre-Trump G.O.P.
shortDescription: In the struggles over the speakership, the old G.O.P. world, with all its dysfunctions, stalemates and futility, has come again.By Ross Douthat

title: Schumer taps Peters again as Senate campaign chief in hopes of 2022 repeat
shortDescription: The second-term senator successfully defended Democrats’ majority last cycle under difficult circumstances, with the party even picking up one more seat in the chamber.
